changes: CI lint errors fixed: removed debug console.log blocks, fixed type issues and unused code in radialLayout.ts, treeTransformer.ts, elkDslExport.ts, and index.tsx.
Sunburst-Beschriftungen verwenden jetzt eine gemeinsame, winkelbasierte Orientierung ohne kopfstehende Texte in Ansicht, SVG und PDF.
Die Sunburst-Darstellung bietet jetzt die getrennte Farbwahl Segment, Gruppe oder Gruppentyp; fehlende Gruppenfarben fallen auf die Gruppentyp-Farbe zurück.
Die maximale Tiefe begrenzt jetzt auch die sichtbaren Sunburst-Ringe korrekt; Ebene 1 entspricht Ring 1.
Sunburst-Hover-Popups zeigen jetzt alle sichtbaren Obergruppen und erlauben, jede davon per Kontextmenü als Startgruppe zu setzen; Obergruppen bleiben nach der Auswahl sichtbar.
Eine gesetzte Sunburst-Startgruppe liegt jetzt immer im Donut-Zentrum und nicht mehr im ersten Ring.
Sunburst-Segmente orientieren ihre Farbe nun an der Ring-1-Wurzel und werden pro Ebene heller.
Sunburst-Segmentgrenzen werden mit einer duennen weissen Linie getrennt.
Die Minimap zeigt im Sunburst-Modus jetzt die Segmente statt einer flachen Knoten-Vorschau.
Sunburst-Beschriftungen passen ihre Zeilenumbrueche und Schriftgroesse jetzt genauer an die verfuegbare Segmentflaeche an.
Sunburst-Farbvererbung orientiert sich jetzt robust am ersten sichtbaren Astsegment ausserhalb des Donut-Zentrums.
Gruppenfarben aus ChurchTools werden jetzt bevorzugt aus dem gelieferten Farbnamen uebernommen und nur bei unbekannten Werten durch die Gruppentyp-Palette ersetzt.
Das Sunburst-Donutloch skaliert wieder mit der Ringbreite ueber einen Radius von Ringbreite/2.
Sunburst-PDF-Exporte versuchen jetzt einen A3-Vektorexport ueber svg2pdf.js statt des bisherigen Rasterbilds.
Der Sunburst-Vektor-PDF-Export verwendet fuer die Beschriftungen jetzt eine serifenlose PDF-Standardschrift.
Sunburst-Beschriftungen werden im SVG/PDF-Export leicht kleiner gesetzt, damit sie in PDF-Fonts sauberer in die Segmente passen.
Die Sunburst-SVG-Erzeugung bleibt zentral an einer Stelle und steuert PDF-spezifische Font-Optionen jetzt nur noch ueber Export-Parameter ein.
HTML-Exporte enthalten jetzt wieder Hover-Popups und verlinken die exportierten Gruppen direkt nach ChurchTools.
Die lokale Entwicklung verwendet verbindlich den Vite-Standardport 5173.
Organigramm wird nun auch ohne Berechtigung "administer persons" gerendert (ohne Leiter-Namen)
GroupMembers-Query wird gar nicht erst gestartet, wenn die Berechtigung fehlt - keine unnoetigen API-Aufrufe
Globaler Retry-Schutz: 403 Forbidden wird nie retried (fuer alle Queries)
Neuer Schalter "Leiter anzeigen" in den Darstellungsoptionen zum Ein-/Ausblenden der Mitglieder auf den Karten
Warnungs-Banner zeigt nur noch an, dass Personendaten fehlen, wenn Leiter angezeigt werden sollen
Neuer Sunburst-Layoutmodus mit d3-hierarchy-basierter Segmentberechnung fuer grosse Hierarchien
Sunburst rendert direkt im bestehenden WebGL/Canvas-Pfad mit Hover, Hit-Testing und Tooltip-Pfaden
Mehrfach-Obergruppen werden im Sunburst ueber eine primaere Obergruppe mit ChurchTools-Feld oder Fallback-Regel aufgeloest
Sunburst-Beschriftungen sind groesser, koennen auf zwei Zeilen umbrechen und Geschwistersegmente unterscheiden sich farblich staerker.
Sunburst nutzt ein kleineres Donutloch und mehr Flaeche fuer groessere Beschriftungen.
Der Ringbreiten-Regler beeinflusst im Sunburst sichtbar die Breite der Hierarchieebenen bei konstantem Zwischenraum.
Auch enge Sunburst-Segmente zeigen nun skalierbare Kleinschrift, die beim Hineinzoomen besser lesbar wird.
Sunburst-Beschriftungen nutzen eine groessere Skalierung fuer bessere Lesbarkeit.
Beschriftungen grosser Sunburst-Segmente folgen nun dem Kreisbogen; schmale Segmente bleiben radial lesbar.
Der Ringbreiten-Regler behaelt beim Neuberechnen die Kameraansicht bei und wirkt dadurch sichtbar.
Der nicht aussagekraeftige Ringbreiten-Regler wird im Sunburst-Modus nicht mehr angezeigt.
Sunburst-Ringe liegen nun ohne Zwischenraum nahtlos aneinander.
Untergruppen fuellen nun den Winkel ihrer Obergruppe vollstaendig aus.
Die Sunburst-Ansicht kann ueber die bestehenden Exportfunktionen als Vektor-SVG und als hochaufloesendes PDF exportiert werden.
Sunburst-Beschriftungen werden im SVG-Export korrekt ausgegeben.
Sunburst-SVGs enthalten Hover-Tooltips mit dem vollstaendigen Hierarchiepfad.
Die aktuelle Ansicht kann als eigenstaendige HTML-Datei exportiert und direkt gehostet werden.
CRITICAL FIX: FLAT_RADIAL edges now form linear chains along rays
Rewrote edge generation to connect nodes sequentially by distance within each ray
Instead of connecting parent to all children, now node[i] → node[i+1] sequentially
Rays now display as proper linear hierarchies: root → child1 → child2 → child3...
Fixed the issue where all nodes connected directly to the first node on each ray
Added ELK DSL export feature for FLAT_RADIAL debugging
Ray structure export shows all nodes grouped by angle with distances from root
ELK DSL graph exported to console for visual analysis
Debug data available as window.__ELK_DSL and window.__RAY_STRUCTURE
Helps identify ray composition and parent-child relationships for troubleshooting
FIXED: FLAT_RADIAL now only shows edges along rays (parent-child on same ray)
Removed cross-ray edges - each ray is an independent hierarchy
Only nodes with matching ray angles create edges (angleThreshold = 9°)
Simplified edge visualization to show only direct parent-child relationships within rays
Cleaner, less cluttered sunburst diagram
FIXED: Reverted to true ray-based positioning for FLAT_RADIAL
Each ray now has same angle, with nodes stacked sequentially by distance from root
Nodes within a ray are evenly spaced along the ray direction (cumulative ringDistance)
Parent-child edges now properly follow rays as they connect nodes on the same angle
Sunburst now displays as proper radial tree with clear ray structure
FIXED: FLAT_RADIAL edges now curve along rays instead of cutting through center
Converted cartesian positions to polar coordinates to determine ray angles
Added bezier control points that pull edges toward center while maintaining ray direction
Edges on same ray now follow the angular direction instead of direct path
Improved visual clarity of parent-child relationships in sunburst layout
FIXED: FLAT_RADIAL edges now render in WebGL canvas
Added section data to edges (required by WebGL renderer)
Each edge now has startPoint, endPoint, and bendPoints for proper rendering
Parent-child relationships are now visually connected along rays
Added ray-based edge connections for FLAT_RADIAL layout
Parent-child relationships are now visualized as edges along each ray
Edges are generated from childrenMap to preserve tree hierarchy in sunburst visualization
Both parent and child must be visible for an edge to be rendered
Improved visual clarity of organizational relationships in sunburst layout
FIXED: FLAT_RADIAL sunburst layout now uses true equidistant positioning
All nodes at the same depth now share the same radius and are distributed angularly around the circle
Replaced per-ray cumulative spacing with global depth-based grouping for perfect sunburst visualization
Nodes are now evenly spaced at each radial layer (depth 1, depth 2, etc.)
Improved debug output to show angle and radius for each node placement
Completed ELK Radial integration with full tree structure support
elkRadial now receives a properly reconstructed childrenMap built from full hierarchies
Added ELK radial-specific layout options (radius=300, compactionSteps=4) for optimal sunburst spacing
elkRadial generates relations only between visible nodes, preserving complete tree paths for layout calculation
Verified both elkRadial and FLAT_RADIAL paths are fully functional with hidden node handling
FIXED: Nodes in each ray are now sequentially numbered (1, 2, 3, ...) regardless of hierarchy
TreeVisitor uses a mutable counter that increments only when adding visible nodes to the ray
Each ray now correctly displays: Ray start=depth1, second visible node=depth2, third=depth3, etc.
Hidden/filtered intermediate nodes are traversed but do NOT consume a depth number
CRITICAL FIX: Depth counter now only increments for VISIBLE nodes in the ray
Hidden/filtered intermediate nodes are traversed but do NOT increase depth
Example: Root → [hidden A] → B (visible) → C (visible) now correctly gives B=depth1, C=depth2
This fixes the core issue where all children of filtered nodes appeared at the same depth
Fixed TreeVisitor to support hidden/filtered intermediate nodes in path traversal
TreeVisitor now creates stub nodes for invisible nodes to preserve complete tree paths
Depth is now calculated across ALL nodes (visible and hidden) to ensure correct positioning of visible descendants
Only VISIBLE nodes are added to the ray output, but traversal continues through hidden nodes to maintain correct depths
This fixes the issue where filtered-out intermediate nodes caused all their children to have the same depth
Fixed FLAT_RADIAL hierarchy reconstruction: now builds childrenMap from full hierarchies instead of filtered relations
This preserves complete tree structure even when intermediate nodes are filtered, ensuring correct path-based depths
Removed unnecessary nodeDepthMap complexity - depth is now correctly determined by each node's position in its ray
Each ray independently traverses from root to its direct child, with nodes appearing at correct depths per path
Fixed TreeVisitor to support DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph) with multiple parents per node
Implemented path-based DFS traversal where each ray independently tracks its own path from root
Added cycle detection per ray to handle nodes reachable via multiple paths with different depths
Nodes with multiple parents now correctly appear in multiple rays with accurate depth-in-ray for each path
TreeVisitor now tracks visitedInRay per ray to ensure correct depth calculation along each unique path
Enhanced TreeVisitor debug logging to show direct children count and actual traversal depth
Added comprehensive childrenMap structure validation logging to diagnose hierarchy flattening issues
Added relation count comparison (parent-child relations vs edges) to identify data structure mismatches
Improved TreeVisitor visitor tracking with per-node child enumeration for debugging depth calculation
Refactored FLAT_RADIAL layout to use TreeVisitor pattern for explicit tree transformation
Implemented Depth-First Search (DFS) traversal that groups descendants into rays, each starting from a direct child of root
Fixed depth calculation within each ray: depths now correctly reflect position within the subtree, not absolute depth from root
Improved layout positioning: nodes on the same ray are now positioned with cumulative radius based on node heights
Enhanced readability by filtering out edges to/from root node in FLAT_RADIAL mode
Added comprehensive debug logging for tree transformation and ray positioning
Added FLAT_RADIAL layout as a new separate layout option (distinct from ELK:Radial)
FLAT_RADIAL creates a sunburst-style visualization with a central root group and all other groups flattened into a single concentric ring
Implemented custom trigonometric positioning for FLAT_RADIAL (x = ringDistance * cos(angle), y = ringDistance * sin(angle))
Added dynamic ring distance slider in the sidebar to adjust FLAT_RADIAL layout spacing (300px - 500px)
Visual hierarchy in FLAT_RADIAL is maintained through node scaling by depth (deeper nodes appear smaller)
FLAT_RADIAL uses optimized custom calculation instead of ELK worker for faster lightweight layout computation
Fixed PDF export when filters hide all groups by generating an informative fallback page instead of producing an invalid document
Added bounds validation in PDF export to prevent division-by-zero and Infinity scaling errors
Removed a redundant layout calculation by using global layout-state in MainComponent instead of calling the reflow hook twice
Made global layout loading state concurrency-safe by tracking active layout jobs across multiple useGenerateReflowData hook instances
Removed unnecessary local state update from reflow effect cleanup to avoid updates during hook disposal/unmount
Added controlled error handling for failed layout runs to avoid unhandled promise rejections while reliably clearing loading flags
Fixed a maximum update depth loop by keeping GraphView mounted during layout recalculation and showing a non-blocking overlay instead
Prevented current node/edge selection from being cleared by pan gestures by suppressing click-selection changes after drag
Added subgraph highlighting: clicking a node highlights it and all its descendants with a blue glow, dimming unrelated nodes and edges
Added subgraph collapsing: right-click a node to toggle visibility of its entire subtree
Added keyboard navigation: arrow keys to pan the canvas, +/- keys to zoom in and out
Implemented dark mode support for the high-performance WebGL renderer
Added theme-aware coloring for nodes, edges, grid, and ports
Updated the minimap to respect the active application theme
Optimized node texture caching to automatically refresh when switching between light and dark modes
Fixed a UI issue where the grid would flash for a fraction of a second during initial render
Removed React Flow renderer to focus exclusively on high-performance WebGL rendering
Cleaned up codebase by removing all reactflow dependencies and types
Simplified renderer selection logic as only WebGL remains
Removed SVG export as it was specific to React Flow and needs reimplementation for WebGL
Optimized sidebar for mobile devices by making it a floating hideable component with a toggle button
Implemented responsive UI that automatically hides floating headers and minimaps on mobile when the sidebar is active, while maintaining visibility on large screens
Refined layout by positioning the sidebar toggle button as a Floating Action Button in the bottom right corner
Relocated zoom and view controls to the bottom left for improved thumb-reachability on mobile
Implemented multi-touch support for canvas interaction, including pinch-to-zoom, improved mobile panning, and double-tap to zoom
Major architectural migration from Create React App (CRA) to Vite and Bun
Upgraded to React 19 and TypeScript 5
Integrated Tailwind CSS v4 with @tailwindcss/vite
Migrated state management to Zustand v5
Implemented TanStack Query v5 for robust data fetching and persistence
Added support for persistent user settings (excluded groups, roles, group types, layout algorithm, start group) using local storage
Redesigned sidebar with collapsible sections and improved exclusion filters
Added Orphaned Groups Wizard to identify and manage disconnected groups
Switched to ELK.js as the primary layout engine for superior graph rendering
Added SVG export functionality for high-quality vector graphics
Refined GraphML export for better compatibility with external graph tools
Implemented URL synchronization for sharing specific graph views and settings
Modernized UI with Radix UI primitives and Lucide icons
Implemented dark mode support throughout the application
Modernized development environment with Flat ESLint Config, Prettier 3, and Husky
Added automated deployment scripts and GitHub Actions for CI/CD
